summary: sensivity installs disguised Windows persistence and runs an obfuscated eval payload at load
details: On require()/load, launcher.js automatically installs a Windows Run-key entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' (impersonating Microsoft OneDrive) under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run, pointing at wscript.exe + a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs that re-launches `node launcher.js` at every login. The same load path spawns a detached, hidden-window supervisor process (windowsHide:true, SENSIVITY_SUPERVISOR=1) that kills anything on TCP 3000, restarts itself on exit, and disguises its window title as 'Runtime Broker'. launcher.js then `eval`s a 150 KB obfuscator.io-protected server.obf.js (RC4 string-array + control-flow flattening) which loads a 6.8 MB native addon (sens.node) containing GTA V / FiveM cheat primitives ('Freecam', 'Teleport', 'spawnVehicle', '*GTAProcess*'), runs PowerShell UI-Automation against Chrome/Edge/Opera/Brave to scrape the URL bar for the hardcoded YouTube video ID 'wJWta2lO0Lw' as an attacker-chosen activation/kill-switch, and POSTs a SHA-256 HWID (MachineGuid + hostname + C: volume serial) plus license key to an obfuscation-concealed `https://iz...` endpoint. The published package description ('Sensivity Control Panel') does not disclose persistence, the hidden supervisor, the URL-bar surveillance, or the game-cheat native module. The combination of Microsoft-impersonating autostart, hidden self-restarting daemon, obfuscated eval loader, and external-signal activation is a backdoor, not a legitimate tool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
